context: Checkpoint 2026-04-29 after pushed CI-green streaming consumer polish. Prior host-proxied LLM streaming stack remains live opt-in via LLM_STREAM_RESPONSES=1; credentials and route enforcement stay host-owned. This checkpoint focused on sharing/pushing, CI validation, and client/CLI consumption of partial AgentResponse chunks.
current_tasks: Pushed the large streaming/usage/validation-economy stack to origin/develop. CI run 25133074023 passed.

Added Tractor CLI plain-mode streaming polish: partial AgentResponse chunks print as deltas, stdout flushes after each delta, and the final full content is not duplicated when partials were already printed. JSON output remains event-based for structured consumers. Commits: dce07a0c, 2a119c4c, ae8a45cf, d08ced8d, af69feb6.

Added TypeScript AgentResponse stream accumulation utility in @refarm.dev/tractor: applyAgentResponseStreamEvent, reduceAgentResponseStreamEvents, and prompt-grouped reduceAgentResponseStreamEventsByPrompt with UNKNOWN_AGENT_RESPONSE_PROMPT_REF fallback. Exported from node and browser entrypoints. Commits: d95f4d70, 573cee3e, 96965683, e671d0bc.
Pushed CLI/tractor-ts consumer polish stack. CI run 25135215147 passed. Pushed prompt-grouped accumulator follow-up. CI run 25135898891 passed.
Updated docs/agent-streaming.md and packages/pi-agent/ROADMAP.md to document CLI plain output behavior and TypeScript accumulator utilities.

Validation used: npm run agent:streaming:check; npm run agent:streaming:harness; cargo check --quiet in packages/tractor; cargo test --bin tractor plain_output --quiet; npm --prefix packages/tractor-ts run type-check; npm --prefix packages/tractor-ts run test:unit -- agent-response-stream.test.ts; npm run project:validate --silent; git diff --check; GitHub Actions watch with --exit-status.
Ran npm run clean:light at checkpoint. Disk still tight: about 7.2GB free; Rust targets about 4.9GB.
blockers: Disk remains tight (~7.2GB free after clean:light). Avoid global/full builds unless preparing a gate; clean:light removes incremental caches so use only at checkpoints/low disk.

Pre-push hook continues to warn that scoped tractor/tractor-ts unit tests fail or time out locally, but targeted local checks and GitHub Actions passed. Full tractor-ts suite remains noisy due fixtures; use targeted tests unless working on fixtures.
pi-lens/LSP monitor still crashes on some Rust/Markdown edits with memory access out of bounds; does not correlate with cargo/ts failures.
Streaming remains opt-in only via LLM_STREAM_RESPONSES; default buffered path remains unchanged.
next_actions: If continuing streaming lane, verify actual WebSocket/UI consumers use the TypeScript accumulator or equivalent sequence/is_final handling.
Consider an integration example or small client smoke for JSON-mode AgentResponse accumulation if a concrete WebSocket client surface is available.
Consider broader provider-specific streamed usage parsing only when concrete provider fixtures demand it (Anthropic cache tokens, OpenAI stream_options include_usage edge cases).
Consider shared target-neutral provider stream parser extraction only if duplication between pi-agent provider_runtime/stream_events.rs and Tractor llm_stream_events.rs starts drifting.
Do not edit .github/workflows without explicit confirmation; CI wrappers for agent:streaming scripts remain optional/protected-surface work.
open_questions: Should LLM_STREAM_RESPONSES become a CLI/config option instead of env-only?
Should the long-term host persistence shape remain direct partial AgentResponse nodes, or introduce generic StreamChunk with AgentResponse projection?
Do WebSocket clients need a first-class protocol event for partial/final state, or is CRDT AgentResponse observation sufficient?
Should prompt-grouped accumulator handle out-of-order sequences defensively, or rely on storage/query ordering plus sequence sorting by consumers?
key_decisions_pending: Generic StreamChunk vs direct AgentResponse partials.
Shared parser extraction vs current duplicated parser logic.
Provider-specific usage accounting breadth for SSE streams.
Client UX completion criteria for declaring streaming polished.
